title: Privacy
intro: Helexa is built so that your data stays with you. This page describes, completely, what is stored where and why. It is short because there is little to disclose.

summary.title: The short version
summary.noCookies: No cookies. No analytics. No trackers. No advertising.
summary.localHistory: Your chat history is stored only in your browser. It is never sent to, or stored on, our servers beyond the moment of generating a reply.
summary.noAnonId: Anonymous visitors are not fingerprinted, profiled or identified.

local.title: What is stored in your browser
local.intro: Everything below stays on your device, exists solely to make the product work, and can be removed at any time by clearing this site's data in your browser.
local.item1: Chat history, projects and message drafts — the product's core promise is that these never leave your browser.
local.item3: A session marker for the beta notice you dismissed.
local.item4: If you sign in: your session token, and — only if you explicitly enable it — an API key for chatting from this device.
local.item5: An anonymous message counter that enforces the free-usage cap.

account.title: If you create an account
account.intro: Accounts exist for metering and abuse prevention, not profiling. Server-side we store:
account.item1: Your email address (for sign-in, verification and password reset) and a salted password hash — never the password itself.
account.item2: Usage metering: token counts per request, needed to enforce budgets and compensate the operators who serve your requests. Prompt and response content is not retained.
account.item3: At registration only, a one-time browser signal (a fingerprint hash) used to detect multi-account abuse of the free tier. It is not used for tracking, is never computed for anonymous browsing, and plays no further role after registration.

infra.title: Infrastructure
infra.body: Inference runs on independent European operators' hardware under European law. The public website is fronted by Cloudflare, which may set a strictly-necessary security cookie of its own (e.g. bot mitigation) that we neither read nor use.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: Under the GDPR you can request access to, correction of, or deletion of your account data at any time, and you can lodge a complaint with your supervisory authority. Deleting your browser data removes your chat history; deleting your account removes everything we hold server-side.
